title: Shawarma-Spiced Chicken Pitas + Cucumber-Tomato Salad + Yogurt Sauce
ingredients: 1 tsp paprika (smoked if you have it)
Optional: tahini (stir 1 tsp into the sauce for extra richness)
instructions: Make the yogurt sauce: In a bowl mix 1 cup Greek yogurt + 1 minced garlic clove + 1 tbsp lemon juice + pinch of salt. Optional: stir in 1 tsp tahini. Set aside.
Make the salad: Dice cucumber and tomatoes. Thinly slice red onion if using. Toss with 1 tbsp lemon juice, a pinch of salt, and 1 tbsp olive oil. Set aside (it gets better as it sits).
Season the chicken: Pat chicken dry. In a bowl toss with 1 tbsp olive oil + 1 tsp cumin + 1 tsp paprika + (optional 1/2 tsp turmeric + 1/2 tsp coriander) + 1/2 tsp salt + pepper.

Cook chicken (stovetop): Heat a large skillet over medium-high. Add chicken in a single layer. Cook 4–6 min per side until browned and cooked through. Doneness cue: center is no longer pink; thermometer: 165°F. Transfer to a board and rest 3 minutes, then slice.
Warm pitas: Wrap pitas in a damp paper towel and microwave 20–30 seconds, OR warm in a dry skillet 30–45 sec per side.
Assemble: Spread yogurt sauce inside pita, add sliced chicken, top with salad (and greens if using).
Kid-friendly notes: keep spices mild (use less paprika/cumin); serve components separately so kids can build their own. Common mistake: don’t crowd the pan or chicken will steam instead of brown.
notes: Bright, savory, and customizable. Great leftovers as a bowl the next day.
